mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.22% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 7.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations contract.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances where UNRATE > 7.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 346810M and HOUST at 466K units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST contraction, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 101.0 signals supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 101.0 and remains there for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 101.337 is near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO < 101.0 for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumer discretionary earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 133768K and UNRATE at 8.1%
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint employment decline and unemployment rise, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in real GDP (GDPC1) above trend signals strong aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11525.0 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL growth > 3% annualized) signals monetary policy lag that creates financial repression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as real yields compress and equity risk premiums decline.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL growth > 3% annualized over prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.22% and CPIAUCSL at 212.174
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that reduces productive capacity and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk premia widen.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 101.337 and PAYEMS at 133768K
regime_hint: synchronized supply-labor shock or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint INDPRO and PAYEMS contraction, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
